_source: TSU Fast Track Weeks 1-6, TSI Day 2, TSU Connect training
_description: Call scripts, voicemail templates, and outreach sequence guidance for TSI sellers

phases.purpose: Identify yourself quickly — name and company only
phases.guidance: No long intro. No 'How are you today?' Just: 'Hi, this is [Name] with Townsquare Ignite — I'll be brief.'

phases.purpose: Why you're calling THIS business TODAY — specific, not generic
phases.guidance: Reference something real about their business — their category, local reputation, a visible opportunity, or seasonal timing. Show you've done research.

phases.purpose: One sentence that creates curiosity without pitching a product
phases.guidance: Reference a result you've seen with a similar business or a local insight — not a product name. Keep it outcome-focused.

phases.purpose: Clear, specific request for a 20-minute meeting
phases.guidance: Give two specific day options. Sound like someone who values their time and yours. Don't ask 'Would you be interested in learning more?' — ask for the meeting.

phone_script_templates.standard_cold_call: Hi, this is [Your Name] with Townsquare Ignite — I'll be quick. I was doing some local research on [category] businesses in [market] and [Business Name] stood out. I had a specific idea I wanted to run by you around [business outcome — e.g. 'getting more people through the door during your busy season' / 'making sure you show up when people in [market] search for [service]']. Would you have 20 minutes this [Day] or [Day]? I'll come prepared with something specific for your business.
phone_script_templates.referral_mention: Hi, this is [Your Name] with Townsquare Ignite. I work with a few businesses in [category] in the area — [reference a client if permissioned] — and I had a specific idea I wanted to share with you. Can I take 20 minutes of your time on [Day] or [Day]?
phone_script_templates.follow_up_call_after_email: Hi [First Name], this is [Your Name] from Townsquare Ignite — I sent you a brief email earlier this week. I wanted to follow up personally because I had a specific idea for [Business Name] I think is worth a quick conversation. Do you have 20 minutes [Day] or [Day]?
phone_script_templates.second_follow_up: Hi [First Name], [Your Name] again from Townsquare Ignite. I've reached out a couple of times — I'll keep it simple. I have a specific idea for [Business Name] around [growth angle]. If there's any interest at all, I'd love 20 minutes. If the timing is off, I completely understand — just let me know either way.
phone_script_templates.breakup_call: Hi [First Name], [Your Name] from Townsquare Ignite. I'm going to make this my last reach out for a while. I genuinely believe there's an opportunity for [Business Name] around [specific angle], but I don't want to keep showing up uninvited. If things change and you want to explore this down the road, I'm easy to find. Wishing you a great [season/month].

voicemail_templates.first_voicemail: Hi [First Name], this is [Your Name] with Townsquare Ignite. I was doing local research on [category] businesses in [market] and [Business Name] came up — I had a specific idea I think would be relevant to your [busy season / growth goals / recent expansion]. Give me a call at [Your Phone] — I'll keep it brief.
voicemail_templates.second_voicemail: Hi [First Name], [Your Name] from Townsquare Ignite again. I know you're busy — I sent an email too. I have something specific for [Business Name] I've seen work well in your category. [Your Phone] — happy to make it worth your time.
voicemail_templates.final_voicemail: Hi [First Name], last one from me — [Your Name] at Townsquare Ignite. I wanted to reach out one more time about an idea for [Business Name]. If the timing isn't right, completely understood. [Your Phone] if anything changes. Wishing you the best.

walk_in_scripts.standard: Hi — I'm [Your Name] from Townsquare Ignite. I was doing local research today and [Business Name] stood out. I had a quick idea I wanted to share with whoever handles your marketing — is [owner/manager name] available for just a few minutes?
walk_in_scripts.if_gatekeeper: Totally understand — I'll make it easy. I'm [Your Name] from Townsquare Ignite. Could I leave my card and a brief note? I had a specific idea for [Business Name] I think they'd find useful.

linkedin_outreach.connection_request: Hi [First Name] — I work with local businesses in [market] on digital marketing strategy. Came across [Business Name] and thought there might be something worth sharing. Happy to connect.
linkedin_outreach.follow_up_after_connect: Thanks for connecting. I was doing research on [category] businesses in [market] and had a specific idea for [Business Name] around [growth angle]. Would you be open to a quick 20-min call?
linkedin_outreach.character_limit_note: LinkedIn connection messages must be under 300 characters. Keep it simple and human.

step_1_day_1.purpose: Open with a strong VBR. Reference something specific about their business. End with a clear meeting ask.
step_1_day_1.length: Short — 3-4 paragraphs, no more than 150 words

step_2_day_2.purpose: Reference the Day 1 email by name. Keep it under 30 seconds.
step_2_day_2.guidance: Say 'I sent you an email yesterday about [Business Name]' — don't re-pitch, just reference and ask for time

step_3_day_3.purpose: Include a brief, specific success story relevant to their industry or category
step_3_day_3.guidance: One real example with a result — not a generic testimonial. Connect the story to their specific situation.

step_4_day_5.purpose: Brief connection message or comment. Under 150 characters. Casual and human.
step_4_day_5.guidance: Don't pitch on LinkedIn. Connect first. Comment on content if possible.

step_5_day_7.purpose: Reiterate VBR and urgency. Reference any prior interaction if it happened.
step_5_day_7.guidance: Build on what you've already sent — show you've been consistent and specific, not just dialing

step_6_day_9.purpose: Highlight key value and include a reference or testimonial if possible
step_6_day_9.guidance: Use a real client story or stat from TSI. Keep it brief. End with a clear ask.

step_7_day_10.purpose: State this is the last attempt. Warm, slightly humorous, leaves the door open.
step_7_day_10.guidance: Break-up emails often get the highest response rate of the sequence — keep it light, specific, and human

fear_of_loss_language: Your [season / growth window] is [X] weeks out — businesses that start now typically see results when it matters most.
I'm working with a few businesses in [category] in [market] right now — I want to make sure [Business Name] doesn't miss the window.
Spots for our [specific campaign type] in [market] fill up — I want to have this conversation before that changes.
Your competitor in [market] is active right now. I don't know if they're working with us, but I know the category is getting more competitive.

demo_meeting_commitment.before_presenting: Before I walk you through this, I want to make sure it's actually a fit. Can I ask — if I showed you a campaign that [specific outcome], and the math made sense for your business, is this something you'd want to move on?
demo_meeting_commitment.mini_close: Are we in agreement on how we'd measure the success of this? / Do you feel like this approach would actually deliver the results we talked about? / If both of us feel good about this, are you ready to move forward today?
